title: The impact of anxiety and intolerance of uncertainty on climate change distress, policy support, and pro-environmental behaviour
authors: Danielle Goldwert; Amelia S. Dev; Hannah C. Broos; Kenneth Broad; Kiara R. Timpano

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: regulating carbon dioxide as a pollutant
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: A regulation, not a tax instrument.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: provide tax rebates for people who purchase energy-efficient vehicles or solar panels
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is a rebate/incentive, not a carbon or fuel tax.

inclusion_reason: The paper measures general climate policy support and pro-environmental behavior, but it does not analyze acceptability of any carbon, fuel, gasoline, diesel, or fossil-fuel tax.
exclusion_reason: No direct carbon or fuel tax item is analyzed; the policy support measure includes only regulation, renewable mandates, efficiency rules, research funding, and tax rebates.
